steps.title: Identify the parallel structure
steps.content:
The passage first describes a product monopsony (single buyer forces lower prices), then introduces a labor monopsony scenario with a parallel effect (employers force lower wages).

The structure compares two equivalent cases with the same underlying dynamic, signaling that we need a transition showing similarity or parallel relationship.

steps.title: Select the transition signaling similarity
steps.content:
Similarly directly indicates that labor monopsony operates parallel to product monopsony—just as the single buyer forces lower prices in one case, employers force lower wages in the other.

Earlier implies a time sequence not present in the text; Instead suggests contrast that contradicts the parallel structure; and In particular treats labor monopsony as a specific example rather than a parallel case.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
